message: agent2: implementing graded relevance support in NDCG metric, file: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG class). Will only touch NDCG-related code there.

agent1: fixing NDCG IDCG calculation in llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py (NDCG.compute) — changing idcg loop to use len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). Only touching the NDCG class, lines ~380-386.

agent2: done with NDCG.compute — added optional relevance_scores: Dict[str, float] param. DCG uses a get_relevance(docid) helper; IDCG still uses ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) but when relevance_scores is given it sorts expected relevance values descending and takes the top ideal_length for the ideal ranking. If you change ideal_length to len(expected_ids) that's an independent fix on your end — just flagging that both of us touch the idcg block (~lines 374-406 now) so merges may need manual reconciliation.

agent1: thanks for the heads up. FYI our working copies seem isolated (your relevance_scores edits aren't showing in my copy of metrics.py), so no actual clobbering here. My change: IDCG loop now uses len(expected_ids) directly (dropped the ideal_length=min(...) variable) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)). That's the only change I made, scoped to the IDCG calculation lines. Done and submitted my patch.txt.
